system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

vertices

    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17

    // Block 1: Bend section

    // Block 2: Post-bend straight duct

);

);

);

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

Block hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(8 12 13 9) 4(11 10 14 15) 4(8 9 10 11) 4(12 15 14 13) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run3/runs/squareBend/1/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 57.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Same as before — Block 2 `hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)` has inward-pointing faces.
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cascading error from failed mesh generation.

## Root Cause Analysis

The previous attempt correctly identified that the block winding needed reversal. However, the issue with Block 2 (`hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13)`) being "inward-pointing faces" suggests the problem is more subtle than just reversing the entire block order.

Let me analyze the vertices for Block 2:
- Vertices 8(25,0,25), 11(25,-50,25), 10(25,-50,-25), 9(25,0,-25)
- Vertices 12(25,-75,25), 15(25,-125,25), 14(25,-125,-25), 13(25,-75,-25)

All eight vertices have **x = 25**. This means Block 2 is a **degenerate block** — all vertices lie on the same plane (x=25). A hexahedron requires vertices to span a volume (non-zero determinant), but here the block collapses into a flat region. No matter how you permute these vertices, the block remains invalid because the geometry itself is degenerate.

Similarly, Blocks 1 and 2 share the same x-coordinate (25) for their defining vertices, creating another degenerate block.

### Root Cause

The vertex layout places vertices 4-11 all at x=0 and x=25 respectively, meaning Blocks 1 and 2 are geometrically collapsed. Vertex 8 through 15 all have x=25, so Blocks 1, 2, and 3 share the same x-plane vertices, creating degenerate/inverted blocks.

## Solution

The vertex positions need to be restructured so that adjacent blocks connect properly with non-degenerate geometry. The standard squareBend tutorial uses a stepped geometry where the bend transitions between ducts of different cross-sections. Here's the corrected approach:

**Redefine vertices** to create proper block connectivity:

- Move vertices 8-11 to represent the corner properly (not all at x=25)
- Redefine Block 1 as a proper L-shaped transition block connecting the inlet duct (x=-50..0) to the post-bend duct (x=25.....).
- Ensure all blocks span meaningful volumes in 3D space.

Specifically, the bend vertices should be positioned such that:
- Block 1 connects the top/bottom faces of the inlet duct to the side faces of the post-bend duct
- Each block spans a genuine 3D region

If maintaining the exact same geometric shape as the user described (with the bend occurring at x=25), the vertex numbering and block connectivity must be redesigned entirely so that no block has all vertices coplanar. One viable approach is to shift the bend region vertices slightly in the x-direction or restructure the block topology so that each hex spans from one duct section to the next with proper right-hand vertex ordering.

Since the user states "Follow the same geometry as the tutorial," the best course is to adopt the **exact blockMeshDict from the official squareBend tutorial** rather than attempting to hand-craft a working vertex layout. The tutorial's block definitions are known to produce valid meshes.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace the entire vertex list and block definitions with the correct squareBend tutorial geometry; Fix Block 1 hex definition to avoid inward-pointing faces by reordering vertices properly; Restructure vertices so all blocks span valid 3D volumes - move bend vertices to proper positions; Define 4 blocks: inlet duct, bend elbow, post-bend duct, outlet duct with appropriate vertex ordering using right-hand rule; Ensure no block has all vertices coplanar'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
